title: MIXING_OF_MULTIPLE_JETS_WITH_A_CONFINED_SUBSONIC
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

1.  **Mathematical Equations:**
    *   0 = (Tm - T) / (Tm - T₁)   (Eq. 1)

2.  **Nomenclature (Definitions):**

    *   Aj/Am: jet-to-mainstream area ratio; (π/4)/((S/Ho)(Ho/D)²) for one-side injection; (π/2)/((S/Ho)(Ho/D)²) for two-side injection
    *   C: (S/Ho)/(J); Eq. 3
    *   Ca: orifice discharge coefficient
    *   D: orifice diameter
    *   D; (D)(Ca)
    *   DR: jet-to-mainstream density ratio (≈Tm/T;)
    *   dH/dx: duct convergence rate
    *   Heq effective duct height; Ho except for opposed rows of jets with centerlines in-line
    *   Ho: duct height at injection plane
    *   J: jet-to-mainstream momentum-flux ratio (DR)(R)²
    *   M: jet-to-mainstream mass-flux ratio (DR)(R)
    *   n: number of holes around can; see Eq. 6
    *   r: radial coordinate
    *   R: jet-to-mainstream velocity ratio (Vj/Um)
    *   Rei: inner radius of curvature in x-r plane
    *   R,: inner radius of curvature at inlet in r-z plane
    *   S: spacing between orifice centers
    *   Sx: spacing between orifice rows
    *   T: temperature
    *   T, jet exit temperature
    *   Tm: mainstream temperature
    *   U: velocity
    *   Um: mainstream velocity
    *   Vjjet velocity
    *   wj/wm: (DR)J(Ca)(Aj/Am)
    *   wj/wT: jet-to-total mass flow ratio; equilibrium 0
    *   W₁: wj/wm / 1+w/Wm
    *  W±: jet half-widths on injection (-) or opposite (+)side of jet centerline; see Fig. 5
    *   x: downstream coordinate; 0 at injection plane
    *   y: cross-stream(radial) coordinate; 0 at wall; y, at location of minimum temperature in a line x = constant, z = constant
    *   z: lateral (circumferential) coordinate; 0 at center-plane
    *   0: (Tm - T) / (Tm - T;) Eq.1
    *   0min: minimum temperature difference ratio on injection (-) or opposite (+) side of jet centerline; see Fig. 5

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

title: MIXING_OF_MULTIPLE_JETS_WITH_A_CONFINED_SUBSONIC
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Okay, here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images.

**1. Mathematical Equations**

*   θ = wj/wt (equilibrium θ is equal to the fraction of the total flow entering through the dilution jets)

**2. Graphs/Charts**

*   **Figure 2: Experimental mean temperature distributions:**
    *   **Axes:**
        *   X-axis: θ (Dimensionless temperature)
        *   Y-axis: Y/Ho (Dimensionless duct height)
        *   Z-axis: Z/S (Dimensionless spanwise of orifice spacing)
    *   **Curves:** Mean temperature distributions.
    *   **Key values:**
        *   J = 26.2 (Momentum-flux ratio)
        *   S/Ho = 0.5 (Dimensionless orifice spacing)
        *   Ho/D = 4 (Dimensionless duct height to orifice diameter)
    *   **X/H0 labels:** 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2 (Dimensionless axial distance)

**3. Tables**

*   No explicit tables but stated that Table 1 gives the ranges investigated for flow and geometric variables.

**4. Diagrams**

*   **Figure 1: Schematic of multiple jet flow**
    *   Components, upstream to downstream:
        *   Main flow (U∞, T∞)
        *   Orifices labeled S, D
        *   Dilution jets (Vj, Tj)
        *   Annotations:
            *   Ho = 10.16 cm
            *   30.48 cm

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Ho = Duct height at the injection location
*   D = Orifice diameter
*   S = Spacing between adjacent orifices
*   Sx = Axial spacing between rows
*   S/Ho = Ratio of the orifice spacing to duct height
*   Ho/D = Ratio of the duct height to orifice diameter
*   Sx/Ho = Ratio of the axial spacing to the duct height
*   Rei/Ho = Inner radius of curvature of the duct normalized byThe inlet duct height
*   Rt/Ho = Radius of curvature of the inner duct wall normalized by the inlet duct height
*   0 = 0 if T = Tm
*   0 = 1 if T = Tj

Is there anything else I can help with?

title: MIXING_OF_MULTIPLE_JETS_WITH_A_CONFINED_SUBSONIC
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Okay, here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   C = (S/Ho) * sqrt(J)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

Table 1: Ranges of flow and geometric variables on which model is based

| Independent variables:                        | Range     |
| :--------------------------------------------- | :-------- |
| Downstream distance, x/Ho                     | 0-2       |
| Density ratio, DR                              | 0.5-2.2   |
| Momentum-flux ratio, J                         | 5-105      |
| Orifice diameter, Ho/D                          | 4-16      |
| Orifice transverse (circumferential) spacing S/D | 2-6       |
| Orifice row axial offset, Sx/Ho                 | 0.25-0.5  |
| Orifice aspect ratio (long/short)              | 1-2.8     |
| Area ratio (exit/inlet)                       | 0.33-1    |
| Radius of curvature in x-r plane, Rxi/Ho       | 0.25-∞   |
| Radius of curvature in r-z plane, Rzi/Ho       | 0-∞       |
| Variable mainstream θ                          | 0-0.5     |
| Derived variables:                             |           |
| Orifice spacing, S/Ho                           | 0.125-1   |
| A/Am                                             | 0.025-0.1 |
| Wj/WT                                            | 0.075-0.36|

**4. Diagrams:**

Figure 3: Orifice configurations. (Orifice Configurations A through O - Represented by respective geometries.)

For configuration A: Single central circle.
For configuration B: Central circle surrounded by 4 smaller circles.
For configuration C: Central circle with two semi-circular arcs inside the circle.
For configuration D: Filled rectangle in the center.
For configuration E: Ellipse at an angle.
For configuration F: Diagonal dashed/filled ellipse.
For configuration G: Ellipse.
For configuration H: Square.
For configuration I: Large squares and smaller square in the middle.
For configuration J/K: Vertical filled rectangle.
For configuration L: Vertical chain of filled circles.
For configuration M: Central circle with four smaller circles in the diagonals.
For configuration N: Series of small circles vertically aligned.
For configuration O: Two side arcs with an ellipse at the center.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   S/Ho
*   Ho/D
*   Sx/Ho
*   x/Ho
*   DR (Density ratio)
*   J (Momentum-flux ratio)
*   S/D (circumferential spacing)
*   Rx/Ho
*   Rz/Ho
*   θ (Variable mainstream)
*   A/Am
*   Wj/WT

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   C = (S/Ho) * sqrt(J)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

    *   **Plots (a, b, c):**
        *   Y-axis: Y/H0
        *   X-axis: X/Dj and Z/S

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 4: Spacing and momentum-flux ratio relationships**

    | Configuration             | C=(S/H0)sqrt(J) |
    | :------------------------ | :-------------- |
    | Single-side injection:    |                 |
    | Under-penetration         | <1.25           |
    | Optimum                   | 2.5             |
    | Over-penetration          | >5.0            |
    | Opposed rows of jets:    |                 |
    | In-line optimum           | 1.25            |
    | Staggered optimum         | 5.0             |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 8:** Diagrams illustrating mixing of multiple jets. Shows cross-sections of flow fields (oblique profile plots - Y/H0 vs X/Dj and Z/S) and isotherm contours.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   X/Ho = 0.5
*   S/Ho = 1, 0.5, 0.25
*   Ho/D = 4, 8
*   Af/Am = 0.05, 0.025
*   J = 5.3, 28.4, 92.7
*   Slots ratio = 2.8
*   S/Ho = 0.5 and Ho/D = 4

title: MIXING_OF_MULTIPLE_JETS_WITH_A_CONFINED_SUBSONIC
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 38]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   (0min)/(0c) = 1 - exp(-c+)
*   c+ = (a3)(0.038)(J)^1.62 (S/D)^1.5 (Heq/D)^-2.57 / (x/Heq)^1.1
*   a3 = 1 if (Yc/Heq + W/Heq) <= 1
*   a3 = (Ho/Heq)^3.67 if (Yc/Heq + W/Heq) > 1
*   (0min)/(0c) = 1 - exp(-c)
*   c = (Q)(a4)(J)^-0.3 (S/D)^-1.4 (Heq/D)^0.9 (Ca)^0.25 / (x/Heq)^0.9
*   Q = 1 if (Yc/Heq + W/Heq) <= 1
*   Q = exp[(0.22)(x/Heq)^2 ((J^0.5)/5 - S/Heq)] if (Yc/Heq + W/Heq) > 1 or Rci/Heq = infinity
*   a4 = 1.57 if Rci/Heq = infinity (straight duct)
*   a4 = 3.93 if Rci/Heq < infinity (curved duct)
*   (W*1/2)/Heq = (a5)(J)^0.18 (S/D)^-0.25 (Ho/Heq)^0.5 (Ca)^0.125 / (x/Heq)^0.5
*   a5 = 0.1623 if Rci/Heq = infinity (straight duct)
*   a5 = 0.3 if Rci/Heq < infinity (curved duct)
*   (W*1/2)/Heq = (a6)(J)^0.15 (S/D)^0.27 (Heq/D)^-0.38 (Ho/Heq)^0.5 (Ca)^0.055 / (x/Heq)^0.12
*   a6 = 0.2 if Rci/Heq = infinity (straight duct)
*   a6 = 0.5 if Rci/Heq < infinity (curved duct)
*   yc,z = yc * (1 - (4)(z/S)^2 (exp(-g)))
*   g = (0.227)(J)^0.67 (S/D)^-1 (Heq/D)^0.54 (Ca)^0.23 / (x/Heq)^0.54
*   (0c,z)/0c = 1 - (4)(z/S)^2 (exp(-d))
*   ye/Heq = (a1)(0.3575)(J)^0.25 (S/D)^0.14 (Heq/D)^-0.45 (Ca)^0.155 (x/Heq)^0.17 (exp(-b))
*   a1 = min[ (1 + S/Heq), 2]
*   b = (0.091)(x/Heq)^2 [ (Heq/S) - (J^0.5)/3.5 ]
*   θ

title: MIXING_OF_MULTIPLE_JETS_WITH_A_CONFINED_SUBSONIC
text:
